message.content:
To calculate the average speed for the whole trip, we must use the fundamental definition of average speed:

$$ \text{Average Speed} = \frac{\text{Total Distance}}{\text{Total Time}} $$

It is important to note that **total time** includes all time spent traveling as well as any time spent stopped.

### Step 1: Calculate the Total Distance

The trip consists of two travel segments. We sum the distances of these segments.

*   Distance of first segment ($d_1$): $120 \text{ km}$
*   Distance of second segment ($d_2$): $200 \text{ km}$

$$ \text{Total Distance} = d_1 + d_2 $$
$$ \text{Total Distance} = 120 \text{ km} + 200 \text{ km} $$
$$ \text{Total Distance} = 320 \text{ km} $$

### Step 2: Calculate the Total Time

The total time includes the travel time for both segments and the duration of the stop. We must ensure all time units are consistent (we will use hours).

*   Time for first segment ($t_1$): $1.5 \text{ hours}$
*   Time for stop ($t_{\text{stop}}$): $30 \text{ minutes}$
*   Time for second segment ($t_2$): $2 \text{ hours}$

First, convert the stop time from minutes to hours:
$$ t_{\text{stop}} = \frac{30}{60} \text{ hours} = 0.5 \text{ hours} $$
